# Figure description: Gamma VI.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/geophysical sections/Gamma VI.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section (profile)** combining a gravity anomaly curve (upper panel) with a geological-geophysical interpreted cross-section (lower panel). It is a composite figure typical of Soviet-era regional gravity surveys, showing both the measured anomaly profile and the interpreted subsurface geological structure beneath it.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers **Profile VI (Профиль VI)**, extending approximately **600 km** along its horizontal axis (marked in kilometers: 0 to ~600, with graduations at 100 km intervals). The vertical axis of the upper panel shows gravity values in **milligals (мГл)**, ranging from approximately **-400 to +1200 мГл** (with a secondary scale in mgal units on the right side). The lower geological section extends to a depth of approximately **6 km** below the surface. The figure is dated **1984** and is labeled **Чертёж №110, Лист 4** (Drawing No. 110, Sheet 4), page **60**. It is referenced to the **Западно-Тенизская партия (West-Teniz Party)** surveys of **1938–84 гг.** (1938–1984). A library/archive stamp is visible (СССР, inventory number partially legible as **~16…**).

---

## Key Content

### Upper Panel — Gravity Anomaly Curve:
- A continuous **Bouguer gravity anomaly curve** is plotted along the profile length.
- The curve shows a **dramatic negative anomaly** in the western/left portion of the profile (0–~150 km), dropping to approximately **-400 мГл**, suggesting a deep basin or thick low-density sedimentary sequence.
- Moving eastward (~150–300 km), the curve rises sharply back toward zero and into positive territory.
- From ~300–600 km, the anomaly remains **broadly positive**, ranging from ~+100 to over +1200 мГл toward the far right (east), with several **local highs and lows** superimposed. The rightmost portion shows a steep upward climb to maximum values, likely indicating approach to a dense crystalline basement uplift or a major structural high.
- Several **circled observation points** (marked with ⊗) are plotted along the curve at labeled stations (А1, А2, А3, and others), indicating gravity measurement benchmarks or control points.
- A horizontal **zero datum line** is clearly marked.

### Lower Panel — Interpreted Geological Cross-Section:
- The section is divided into **6 named segments or blocks** (numbered км 0 through ~6 on the depth axis), corresponding to depth slices.
- Geological units are labeled with **density values** in **г/см³ (g/cm³)**, indicating rock densities assigned during interpretation. Values include:
  - **0.00 г/см³, -0.02 г/см³, -0.05 г/см³, -0.08 г/см³** (negative contrasts = low-density sedimentary fill)
  - **+0.04, +0.10, +0.16, +0.25, +0.35 г/см³** (positive contrasts = denser basement rocks)
- Stratigraphic/formation labels visible include **PRₗ бг, PR₂ бг, PE₂ бг** (likely Proterozoic formations — "бг" may denote a specific lithologic unit or local formation abbreviation), and what appears to be **Do** (possibly Devonian).
- The section shows **layered horizontal to sub-horizontal geology** in the central portions, with structural complexity and thickening of sedimentary sections in the left (western) portion, consistent with a basin.
- Density contrast annotations are systematically placed within each interpreted layer block, following standard Soviet gravity modeling methodology.

---

## Text and Labels

**Title:** Профиль VI *(Profile VI)*

**Top left:** 1984 г. *(year 1984)*

**Top right:** Чертёж №110 Лист 4 *(Drawing No. 110, Sheet 4)*; circled number **60**

**Archive stamp (partially legible):** СССР … [Инв. №] 16… *(USSR … Inventory No. 16…)*

**Y-axis (left):** мГл *(milligals)* — scale approximately -400 to +1200
**Y-axis (right):** Secondary scale (likely in mgal or relative units): values include -4, -2, 0, +2, +4, +6, +8, +10, +12, +14

**X-axis:** км 0.0, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600 м.б.б. *(meters above sea level or profile distance in km)*

**Lower section depth axis:** км (depth): 0, +2, +4, -6 (approximate)

**Density values in cross-section (transcribed):**
- 0.00 г/см³, -0.02 г/см³, -0.05 г/см³ / -0.08 г/см³ (sedimentary layers)
- +0.04 г/см³, +0.10 г/см³, +0.16 г/см³, +0.25 г/см³, +0.35 г/см³ (basement units)
- Additional values: +0.06 г/см³, +0.07 г/см³, +0.10 г/см³, +0.12 г/см³

**Formation labels:** PRₗ бг, PR₂ бг, PE₂ бг, Do (partially legible)

**Bottom caption:** К отчету Западно-Тенизской партии за 1938–84 гг. *(To the report of the West-Teniz Party for 1938–84)*

**Bottom text:** Условные обозначения на листе №1 *(Legend/key on Sheet No. 1)*

**Anomaly control points labeled:** А1, А2, А3 (circled, along the curve)

This profile provides a **regional gravity transect (~600 km long)** across a major structural boundary in northern
